features.summary: Deere reported Q1 2022 results with net sales up 5% to $9.6B and net income of $903M ($2.92/share). Despite a 5-week work stoppage, the company outpaced industry performance in most categories. Order books are largely full through fiscal 2022, with strong demand continuing to outstrip supply for the second consecutive year. The company announced new LEAP ambitions targeting 20% equipment operations margins and 10% recurring revenue by 2030, supported by a $150B incremental addressable market opportunity across digitalization, autonomy, automation, and electrification.
features.management_outlook: Management raised full-year net income guidance to $6.7-7.1B, expecting continued strong demand to exceed production capacity through 2022. They anticipate supply chain challenges and semiconductor shortages to persist throughout the year, with the highest material and freight cost pressures in Q2. The company is already seeing interest in model year 2023 products and expects to open those orders in Q2. Management expressed confidence in achieving their new LEAP ambitions, including 20% through-cycle margins by 2030, driven primarily by precision technology growth and business model transformation.
features.risks: Supply chain disruptions and semiconductor shortages continue to constrain production capacity and increase freight costs. Material and freight inflation is expected to peak in Q2, with the company being price-cost negative in Q1. Components with heavy labor content remain in tight supply, requiring ongoing mitigation efforts including multi-sourcing and supplier support. The transition to recurring revenue models may create short-term margin trade-offs as revenue shifts from point-of-sale to subscription-based recognition.
features.key_drivers: Strong agricultural fundamentals with commodity prices supporting farmer profitability and equipment demand. Precision agriculture technologies like See & Spray Ultimate (80% herbicide reduction) and autonomous solutions are driving customer adoption and premium pricing. The company's smart industrial strategy reorganization around customer production systems is enabling deeper market penetration. Construction and forestry markets benefit from strong housing, oil and gas activity, and rental company CapEx, while forestry sees elevated lumber production driving 10-15% industry growth.
